description_long: This house features a steeply pitched gable roof with textured shingles and prominent stone chimney, evoking a medieval or Tudor Revival style. The exterior walls are a mix of timber framing and plaster, with exposed wooden beams creating a distinctive half-timbered pattern. Blue-paned windows with lattice designs are arranged in small groups, some set into dormers on the roof. The foundation and entryway are constructed from rough stone, with a small set of steps leading to a wooden door beneath a covered porch. Weathered wooden planks and irregular roof tiles add to the rustic, aged appearance. The overall structure is compact, vertical, and richly detailed, suggesting a historic urban dwelling.
description: A compact, steep-roofed house with half-timbered walls, blue lattice windows, and a stone chimney, evoking a medieval or Tudor Revival style.
description_view_0: Front view shows the symmetrical gable roof, central chimney, and two rows of blue-paned windows with timber framing.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the steep shingled roof with two dormer windows and the stone chimney extending above the roofline.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the main entryway with stone steps, a wooden door, and a covered porch, as well as additional timber framing details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displays a single dormer window, the stone chimney, and the continuation of the half-timbered wall pattern.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with small grouped and dormer windows
attribute_entryway_design: Stone steps lead to a wooden door beneath a small covered porch, with rough stonework and timber framing accenting the entry.
